'''Scalts''' were creatures apparently hailing from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. They evolved into scavengers, and could be found across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] on [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. They featured large air sacks, clubbed tails, and emitted a high-pitched noise for which they were named.

==Biology and appearance==
The front of the Scalt featured large air sacks, and their body ended in a clubbed tail. From their sacks to the tip of their tail, they were often 2½ to 3 feet in length. The Scalts had four [[Limb/Legends|legs]], and could use all four of them for walking, or two of them if they so chose. Scalts also possessed the ability to lay eggs. Their skin was predominantly [[Color/Legends|yellow]], although they featured green mottling on their underbellies.<ref name="daa" />

==Behavior==
Scalts were so named for the ear-piercing noise they emitted. They were eventually given to the lifestyle of a scavenger. They preferred to move about in a bipedal fashion. Scalts were termed as being "hyper" creatures, and were also considered to be highly strung.<ref name="daa" />

==History==
Scalts were believed to have originated on the arid world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. They eventually evolved into scavengers, and could be found on [[Starship/Legends|starships]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition was launched, asking ''[[Star Wars]]'' fans to create artwork depicting completely original aliens inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. In [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]), the winners were announced. In the "honorable mentions" section of the "under 16" division, the Scalt, a creation of 14-year-old [[Mary Burroughs]], was published. ''[[Star Wars]]'' continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that the published material from the "Design an Alien" competition was to be considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> However, the Scalts have yet to be referenced in any other later ''Star Wars'' materials.
